oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Legislative Update, AFA Partnership with Pet Advocacy Network
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Olmos, Jodie
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: We had a very busy year in 2025 fighting various animal legislation in several states. Most bills were animal sale bans in pet shops. There were a total of twenty three state bills, one county ordinance, and two city or-dinances. As of September 1st, 2025, we defeated twenty one of the twenty three bills. While we were at our educa-tional conference in September we defeated a Lincoln County, North Carolina ordinance that would have banned the sale and ownership of exotic pets to include parrots. Our Southeast Regional Director, Teri Rand’s testimony in Lincoln County was key in this victory.Rhode Island introduced a parrot sale ban in pet shops in 2025. Our key to victory in Rhode Island was the testimony of AFA member Matthew Irving, and our President, Buddy Waskey. [For the full article and image, please continue to the PDF]
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 52 No. 2 (2025): AFA Watchbird Fall/Winter 2025; 42-43
